source_note: All 5 individuals verified as U.S. ARMY (not Marines/Navy/Air Force/Coast Guard). Service windows verified to fall within ~last 50 years (Selleck's service ended 1973 — included as a roughly-window edge case per brief's 'roughly' clause). Quotes are verbatim with working source URLs. Photos sourced from Instagram or news/blog articles, not Wikipedia or stock.

people.name: Ice-T (Tracy Marrow)

service.unit: 25th Infantry Division (Tropic Lightning), Schofield Barracks, Hawaii

people.post_army: After his Army discharge, Ice-T pioneered West Coast gangsta rap with albums like Rhyme Pays and O.G. Original Gangster, then transitioned into a multi-decade acting career. He has played Detective Odafin 'Fin' Tutuola on Law & Order: SVU since 2000, becoming one of the longest-serving cast members in scripted TV history.
people.quote: Ice-T joined the Army for the financial benefits and then served 4 years in the 25th Infantry Division.

people.name: Mr. T (Laurence Tureaud)

service.role: Squad leader; named 'Top Trainee of the Cycle' out of 6,000 troops in 1975

people.post_army: After leaving the Army, Mr. T worked as a bouncer and then broke into Hollywood as Clubber Lang in Rocky III (1982) and as B.A. Baracus on The A-Team (1983-1987). His mohawk, gold chains and 'I pity the fool' catchphrase made him one of the most recognizable pop-culture figures of the 1980s.
people.quote: Out of a cycle of 6,000 other troops, Tureaud was elected 'Top Trainee of the Cycle' in 1975. In addition, he received a letter of recommendation from his drill sergeant as well as a promotion to squad leader.

people.name: Jason Everman

service.unit: 2nd Ranger Battalion, 75th Ranger Regiment; later 3rd Special Forces Group (Green Beret)
service.role: Army Ranger, then Special Forces Sergeant First Class; deployed to Iraq and Afghanistan

people.post_army: After being kicked out of both Nirvana and Soundgarden in the late 1980s and early 1990s, Everman enlisted as an infantryman and rose to become a Ranger and then a Green Beret with multiple combat deployments. After leaving the Army he earned a philosophy degree from Columbia University and a master's from Norwich University, becoming the subject of a celebrated 2013 New York Times Magazine profile.
people.quote: Throughout my adult life, I have made the conscious effort to develop three aspects of who I am: the artist, the soldier, and the philosopher. I believe GS has been the ideal place to realize this development.

people.name: J.R. Martinez

service.role: Infantryman / Humvee driver; medically discharged as corporal after IED injury in Karbala, Iraq

people.post_army: After more than 30 surgeries for burns covering 40% of his body, Martinez became a soap-opera actor on All My Children and won Season 13 of Dancing With The Stars in 2011. He is now a New York Times bestselling author, motivational speaker, and one of the country's most recognized advocates for wounded veterans and burn survivors.
people.quote: The day of my injury when I was inside the vehicle and I can literally see my life slipping away, an actual visual of my sister came to me and told me I was going to be OK and told me I'd be fine. And after that sweet whisper went away, they pulled me out of the vehicle and got me immediately into the medical team's hands.

people.name: Tom Selleck

service.unit: 160th Infantry Regiment, California Army National Guard

people.post_army: After the Guard, Selleck became a TV megastar as the lead of Magnum, P.I. (1980-1988), winning an Emmy and Golden Globe. He has continued a six-decade screen career through Three Men and a Baby, Friends, and 14+ seasons as Police Commissioner Frank Reagan on Blue Bloods.
people.quote: I am a veteran; I'm proud of it. I was a sergeant in the U.S. Army infantry, National Guard, Vietnam era. We're all brothers and sisters in that sense.
